LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT TOPA INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO DENY TOPA'S REQUEST TO ALLEGE CLAIMS AGAINST NOVITA INSURANCE SOLUTIONS, LLC AND PATRICIA LOWTHER BECAUSE THOSE CLAIMS ARE NOT COMPULSORY AND ALLOWING THEM WOULD ALMOST CERTAINLY RESULT IN A SIGNIFICANT DELAY OF THE TRIAL. IF THE TRIAL DATE IS CONTINUED TOPA MAY RENEW ITS REQUEST TO ALLEGE CROSS-CLAIMS AGAINST NOVITA AND LOWTHER.. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) ORDER IS SIGNED. VISITING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/HEK)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Cross-Complaint By Defendant Topa Insurance Company
Off Calendar Jan-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
IVO LABAR,, ESQ. SAWYER & LABAR LLP 415.262.3820 labar@sawyerlabar.com Attorney for Plaintiff WINDELER, LLC

Devin T. Shoecraft, Esq. BURTON | KELLEY, LLP (833) 222-8959 Service@burtonkelley.com
Fax: (833) 900-2646 Attorneys for Defendant TOPA INSURANCE COMPANY (via ZOOM)

Jane Hong, CSR #11975 909-831-2644 csrjane@hotmail.com Court Reporter

Hearing held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TOPA INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Cross-Complaint By Defendant Topa Insurance Company

Defendant Topa Insurance Company's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted in part. Topa is granted leave to file a cross-complaint alleging a single cause of action for declaratory relief regarding rescission of the policy it issued to plaintiff Windeler, LLC. That claim is a compulsory cross-claim and Topa has not acted in bad faith as to that claim so it is not precluded from alleging the claim, especially since the claim does not materially differ from several of Topa's affirmative defenses. The court exercises its discretion to deny Topa's request to allege claims against Novita Insurance Solutions, LLC and Patricia Lowther because those claims are not compulsory and allowing them would almost certainly result in a significant delay of the trial. If the trial date is continued Topa may renew its request to allege cross-claims against Novita and Lowther.

Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)
